questions.q: Sushruta is most commonly honoured with which title?
questions.explanation: Sushruta is widely regarded as the 'Father of Surgery' for his detailed surgical treatise.

questions.q: Which ancient medical text is attributed to Sushruta?
questions.explanation: The treatise attributed to Sushruta is the Sushruta Samhita.

questions.q: Sushruta is a foundational figure in which ancient Indian system of medicine?
questions.explanation: The Sushruta Samhita is one of the foundational texts of Ayurveda.

questions.q: Sushruta is especially famous for pioneering which type of surgery?
questions.explanation: Sushruta described reconstructive nose surgery (rhinoplasty), making him a pioneer of plastic surgery.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita describes a surgical procedure for which eye condition?
questions.explanation: Sushruta described an early surgical technique for treating cataracts.

questions.q: Besides operations, the Sushruta Samhita is notable for describing many surgical what?
questions.explanation: The text catalogues hundreds of operations and a large array of surgical instruments.

questions.q: In which broad field did Sushruta make his greatest contributions?
questions.explanation: Sushruta's contributions were in medicine and especially surgery.

questions.q: Sushruta is traditionally remembered as a physician of which ancient land?
questions.explanation: Sushruta was an ancient Indian surgeon and physician.

questions.q: The reconstructive nose surgery described by Sushruta makes him a forerunner of which modern speciality?
questions.explanation: His rhinoplasty techniques make him a forerunner of modern plastic and reconstructive surgery.

questions.q: Roughly how many distinct operations are described in the Sushruta Samhita?
questions.explanation: The Sushruta Samhita describes hundreds of surgical operations.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita is written primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: The Sushruta Samhita is composed in Sanskrit.

questions.q: Sushruta is traditionally said to have taught and practised in which ancient city associated with learning?
questions.explanation: Tradition associates Sushruta with the ancient city of Varanasi (Kashi).

questions.q: Sushruta's rhinoplasty technique reconstructed the nose using a flap of skin taken from where?
questions.explanation: He used a pedicle flap of skin from the cheek or forehead to reconstruct the nose.

questions.q: Which of these surgical concepts is described in the Sushruta Samhita?
options: Classification of surgical instruments and procedures
questions.explanation: The text systematically classifies surgical instruments (yantras and shastras) and procedures.

questions.q: Sushruta is traditionally regarded as a disciple or student of which sage-physician?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Sushruta learned surgery from Dhanvantari, the divine physician.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita recommends that surgical training include practice on what?
options: Models such as fruits, gourds and animal carcasses
questions.explanation: Sushruta advised trainees to practise incisions and procedures on fruits, vegetables, hides and dead animals before operating on patients.

questions.q: Approximately how many surgical instruments (yantras and shastras) does the Sushruta Samhita describe?
questions.explanation: The text describes more than a hundred surgical instruments, including blunt and sharp instruments.

questions.q: Sushruta's rhinoplasty method became known to later European surgeons partly because it was practised and reported from which region?
questions.explanation: The 'Indian method' of nose reconstruction was reported from India and influenced 18th-19th century European surgery.

questions.q: In addition to surgery, the Sushruta Samhita covers detailed knowledge of which subject useful for operating?
questions.explanation: The text contains detailed descriptions of human anatomy, important for surgical practice.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita advised studying anatomy through which method, unusual for its time?
questions.explanation: Sushruta recommended studying the human body through observation of cadavers.

questions.q: Which broad category of disease management is given major emphasis in the Sushruta Samhita compared with other Ayurvedic texts?
questions.explanation: The Sushruta Samhita places its greatest emphasis on surgery, known as Shalya tantra.

questions.q: Which other ancient Indian medical text is most often paired with the Sushruta Samhita as a classical Ayurvedic treatise?
questions.explanation: The Charaka Samhita and Sushruta Samhita are the two great classical foundations of Ayurveda.

questions.q: Sushruta is traditionally believed to have lived around which period?
options: Around the 1st millennium BCE (often placed circa 6th century BCE)
questions.explanation: Sushruta is traditionally dated to the 1st millennium BCE, with many scholars placing him around the 6th century BCE.

questions.q: In Ayurvedic classification, surgery as taught by Sushruta is referred to by which term?
questions.explanation: Surgery in Ayurveda is called Shalya tantra, the branch in which Sushruta specialised.

questions.q: Sushruta's account of nose reconstruction was brought to wider European attention after a description appeared in 1794 in which publication?
questions.explanation: An account of the Indian nose-reconstruction method was published in the Gentleman's Magazine in 1794, helping spread the technique to Europe.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita classifies sharp surgical instruments and blunt instruments under which broad Sanskrit terms respectively?
questions.explanation: Sharp cutting instruments are called shastra and blunt instruments are called yantra in the Sushruta Samhita.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita describes a method of intentionally using which creatures to help drain and clean wounds?
questions.explanation: Sushruta described the medicinal use of leeches (leech therapy) in wound management.

questions.q: Sushruta described eight major kinds of which surgical operation, forming a classic eightfold classification?
options: Types of surgical operation (incision, excision, scarification, puncturing, probing, extraction, evacuation, suturing)
questions.explanation: Sushruta classified surgical procedures into eight kinds: incision, excision, scarification, puncturing, probing, extraction, draining/evacuation, and suturing.

questions.q: The Sushruta Samhita is traditionally divided into a main body plus a later supplementary section known as what?
questions.explanation: The Sushruta Samhita includes a supplementary final section called the Uttara Tantra.

questions.q: For pain relief during surgery, the Sushruta Samhita suggests giving the patient what?
options: Strong herbal preparations and wine to dull pain
questions.explanation: Long before modern anaesthesia, Sushruta recommended wine and herbal preparations to help dull the patient's pain during operations.
